channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Colombia's capital ends drought-related water rationing. Its case is a warning to other cities
item.description: Colombia’s capital has always seemed immune to water scarcity, nestled among cloud-kissed Andean peaks and known for steady rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Astrid Suarez And Steven Grattan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fatou, the world's oldest gorilla in a zoo, is celebrating her 68th birthday in Berlin
item.description: Fatou, the oldest gorilla living in captivity worldwide, is getting ready for her 68th birthday in style.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Razer PC Remote Play lets you stream games to your iPhone – and it could convince me to switch from my gaming PC
item.description: Razer’s PC Remote Play game streaming service lets you play PC games on your iPhone, iPad or Android device.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: I've never been more excited to get my hands on a new handheld: Intel's new graphics driver reportedly provides greater MSI Claw 8 AI+ performance
item.description: Intel's Core Ultra 7 258V processor has taken the MSI Claw AI+ handheld gaming PCs to new heights in terms of performance, and a new graphics driver update has continued the trend.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft study claims AI is still struggling to debug software
item.description: AI is great for generating code, but it’s still underperforming when it comes to simple debugging tasks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Defining leadership through mentorship and a strong network
item.description: Andrea Schulze understands the importance of mentorship when it comes to fostering more gender diversity in the tech industry, especially up to the leadership level.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects
item.description: Microsoft said on Wednesday it is "slowing or pausing" some of its data center projects, describing the move as a show of flexibility as the artificial intelligence (AI) industry evolves.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are demanding President Trump confirm that Elon Musk will leave his role in the administration by May 30. A group of 77 lawmakers, led by Rep.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s BigQuery and Looker get agents to simplify analytics tasks
item.description: Google has added new agents to its BigQuery data warehouse and Looker business intelligence platform to help data practitioners automate and simplify analytics tasks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cisco, Google Cloud offer enterprises new way to connect SD-WANs
item.description: Cisco and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to integrate Cisco’s SD-WAN with the cloud provider’s fully managed Cloud WAN service.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Whistleblower tells senators that Meta undermined US security, interests
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, the former director of global public policy at Facebook, which is now called Meta, testified at a Senate hearing Wednesday that she saw Meta executives “repeatedly undermine U.S. national security and betray American values” during her seven-year stint in that job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: Travelers should prepare for extra scrutiny of devices when crossing borders, especially when entering the U.S. Experts say 100% privacy might be impossible but there things you can do to make it harder for someone to see your private data.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ignoring Wall Street, Apple gets busy on Vision Pro 2.0
item.description: While politicians everywhere seem to be doing their best to make everything pretty difficult, the North Star for Apple remains what it always has been: creating products people seem to love. That’s what got the company through (and out of) tough times before.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Humanoid robot breakdances its way into history
item.description: Atlas robot by Boston Dynamics amazes tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson with its breakdancing moves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Dark Side of AI Benchmarking: Vendors May Fudge Results, But It Won't Dissuade Enterprise Buyers
item.description: The revelation that Meta misled users about the performance of its new Llama 4 model has raised red flags about the accuracy and relevancy of benchmarking in AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet
item.description: Proposed TSA security requirements mark a step in the right direction for ICS/OT security. However, stricter compliance alone isn’t enough. The post For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: China’s rare earth export controls threaten enterprise IT hardware supply chains
item.description: China has announced immediate export controls on seven more rare earth elements critical to enterprise IT hardware manufacturing, firing a fresh salvo in the ongoing tech trade war.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
